The Infrastructure Architect.overview: You thrive in designing and maintaining the foundational systems that everything else runs on. Kubernetes clusters, cloud infrastructure, and IaC templates are your domain. You think in terms of scalability, reliability, and cost efficiency.
The Infrastructure Architect.famousQuote: "Any sufficiently advanced infrastructure is indistinguishable from magic."
The Infrastructure Architect.workStyle: You prefer building systems that scale themselves. You document everything and automate what others do manually. Your PRs often include Terraform modules others reuse for months.

The Pipeline Builder.overview: Your superpower is making code flow smoothly from a developer's commit to production. CI/CD pipelines, Git workflows, and deployment strategies are second nature to you.
The Pipeline Builder.famousQuote: "If deploying scares you, you're not deploying often enough."
The Pipeline Builder.workStyle: You believe in small, frequent deployments. Your pipelines catch issues before humans notice them. You're the person everyone thanks when Friday deploys go smoothly.

The Guardian.overview: Security, monitoring, and reliability are your pillars. You ensure systems are observable, incidents are handled gracefully, and vulnerabilities are patched before they're exploited.
The Guardian.famousQuote: "The best incident is the one your monitoring caught before users noticed."
The Guardian.workStyle: You write runbooks before they're needed. Your dashboards tell stories. When the pager goes off, you're calm — because you've already planned for this.

The Cloud Native.overview: Containers and cloud services are your native language. You think in microservices, service meshes, and managed services. If it runs on Kubernetes, you've probably deployed it.
The Cloud Native.famousQuote: "Why manage servers when they can manage themselves?"
The Cloud Native.workStyle: You containerize everything. Your Helm charts are versioned, your manifests are clean, and your clusters self-heal. You believe in immutable infrastructure.

The Automation Engineer.overview: If a task exists, you've probably automated it. Scripting, IaC, and CI/CD are your tools for eliminating toil and making teams more productive.
The Automation Engineer.famousQuote: "The first time you do it manually, the second time you write a script."
The Automation Engineer.workStyle: You hate repetitive tasks with a passion. Your scripts have scripts. Teams come to you when they want their 3-hour process done in 3 minutes.

The Pixel Perfectionist.overview: You craft interfaces that are visually stunning and meticulously polished. Every pixel matters, every animation is intentional, and accessibility is never an afterthought.
The Pixel Perfectionist.famousQuote: "Good design is obvious. Great design is transparent."
The Pixel Perfectionist.workStyle: You zoom in to 200% to check alignment. Your CSS is clean and semantic. You test on every browser and screen size because you care about every user.

The JS Wizard.overview: JavaScript and TypeScript are extensions of your thinking. You write clean, type-safe code that others can maintain. Complex logic and state management are your playground.
The JS Wizard.famousQuote: "Types are not constraints — they're documentation that never gets stale."
The JS Wizard.workStyle: Your PRs introduce zero `any` types. You refactor with confidence because TypeScript has your back. Complex async flows don't scare you — they excite you.

The Performance Hunter.overview: You live for Core Web Vitals and bundle sizes. Every millisecond counts, and you know exactly where the bottleneck is before others even notice the lag.
The Performance Hunter.famousQuote: "The fastest code is the code that never runs."
The Performance Hunter.workStyle: You profile before you optimize. Lighthouse scores are your KPIs. You've reduced bundle sizes that others thought were already minimal.

The Component Architect.overview: You design component systems that are reusable, composable, and maintainable. Your components work in isolation and compose beautifully together.
The Component Architect.famousQuote: "A good component is one you never have to read the source code to use."
The Component Architect.workStyle: Your components have clear prop interfaces, sensible defaults, and zero surprises. Other developers love using your code because it just works.

The Quality Crafter.overview: You build UIs that are accessible, tested, and bulletproof. Quality isn't something you add at the end — it's baked into every component you write.
The Quality Crafter.famousQuote: "If it's not tested, it's broken — you just don't know it yet."
The Quality Crafter.workStyle: Your components ship with tests. You check screen reader behavior. You catch edge cases in code review that no one else sees.

The Data Whisperer.overview: You think in schemas, queries, and data flows. Database optimization, caching strategies, and data modeling are where you shine.
The Data Whisperer.famousQuote: "Normalize until it hurts, denormalize until it works."
The Data Whisperer.workStyle: You EXPLAIN ANALYZE before shipping. Your indexes are intentional. You know when to cache, when to denormalize, and when to add a read replica.

The API Artisan.overview: You design APIs that other developers love to consume. Clean endpoints, thoughtful error handling, and comprehensive documentation are your hallmarks.
The API Artisan.famousQuote: "A great API is one where the happy path is obvious and the errors are helpful."
The API Artisan.workStyle: Your OpenAPI specs are complete before the first line of code. Your error messages help developers debug. Your endpoints follow consistent naming that just makes sense.

The System Designer.overview: You think in distributed systems, event-driven architectures, and message flows. Scaling challenges excite you, and you can whiteboard a system design in minutes.
The System Designer.famousQuote: "Every system design decision is a trade-off. Know what you're trading."
The System Designer.workStyle: You draw diagrams before writing code. Your services are loosely coupled but highly cohesive. CAP theorem isn't abstract to you — it's a daily consideration.

The Reliability Engineer.overview: Your services stay up when everything else goes down. Circuit breakers, graceful degradation, and observability are your bread and butter.
The Reliability Engineer.famousQuote: "Hope is not a strategy. Redundancy is."
The Reliability Engineer.workStyle: Your services have health checks, circuit breakers, and retry logic. You've read about every outage postmortem you can find. You plan for failure because you know it's inevitable.

The Security Sentinel.overview: You build backends that are secure by default. OWASP top 10, authentication flows, and data protection are always on your mind.
The Security Sentinel.famousQuote: "Security is not a feature. It's a requirement."
The Security Sentinel.workStyle: You review PRs for SQL injection before logic errors. Your auth flows follow OWASP guidelines. Input validation is your first instinct, not an afterthought.

The Test Strategist.overview: You design quality into the process from day one. Test plans, risk analysis, and coverage strategies are how you ensure nothing slips through the cracks.
The Test Strategist.famousQuote: "Quality is not an act, it is a habit."
The Test Strategist.workStyle: You map test cases to requirements. Your test plans cover happy paths, edge cases, and failure modes. Teams trust your assessments because they're thorough and data-driven.

The Automation Ace.overview: You make testing fast, reliable, and repeatable. Automation frameworks, API testing, and CI integration are your specialties.
The Automation Ace.workStyle: Your test suites run in minutes, not hours. Your page objects are reusable. You integrated tests into CI before anyone asked you to.

The Bug Hunter.overview: You have an uncanny ability to find defects that others miss. Exploratory testing, edge case thinking, and thorough bug reports are your trademarks.
The Bug Hunter.famousQuote: "Every bug has a story. Find it, document it, prevent it."
The Bug Hunter.workStyle: You ask 'what if' more than anyone else. Your bug reports are so detailed that developers can reproduce on the first try. You think like a user, not a developer.

The Security Tester.overview: You think like an attacker to protect users. Penetration testing mindset, API security validation, and performance under attack are your focus areas.
The Security Tester.famousQuote: "Test the locks before someone else picks them."
The Security Tester.workStyle: Your test cases include SQL injection attempts and auth bypass scenarios. You read CVE reports for fun. Your API security tests catch what scanners miss.

The Performance Analyst.overview: You know exactly where the system will break under load. Performance benchmarks, bottleneck analysis, and capacity planning are your expertise.
The Performance Analyst.famousQuote: "Performance is a feature. Slowness is a bug."
The Performance Analyst.workStyle: Your JMeter/k6 scripts simulate real user patterns. Your reports include percentiles, not just averages. You've prevented production outages with pre-launch load tests.

The Versatile Engineer.overview: You're balanced across the board — no single weakness, strong everywhere. Your adaptability makes you invaluable in any team and any situation.
The Versatile Engineer.famousQuote: "The best engineers don't just go deep — they go wide enough to connect the dots."
The Versatile Engineer.workStyle: You're the person who can jump between frontend, backend, and infrastructure. Teams love having you because you understand the full picture and communicate across domains.
